data.title: Can Tesla's (TSLA) Model 3 Standard Win Back Europeans? These Are the Obstacles
data.description:
American electric vehicle maker Tesla (TSLA) on Friday launched in Europe a lower-priced standard variant of its popular Model 3 electric sedan. However, the move by the EV maker to reverse its falling sales in the region and compete with cheaper alternatives from Chinese and European rivals faces several obstacles.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

New Variants Strip Feature for Lower Price

The Model 3 Standard is being sold for over $44,000 in Germany, almost $48,000 in Sweden, and more than $32,000 in Norway. The base price of the new variant is about $3500 lower compared to the predecessor's.

The trick is to let go of several features in the older, more expensive versions, including rear screen, ambient lighting, front-seat ventilation, and leather trim. Tesla launched the new variant in October in the U.S.

Earlier in October, the EV maker also introduced a "standard" and more affordable version of its Model Y luxury sport utility vehicle (SUV) across several European markets. The version is about $11,600 cheaper in Germany, for instance, compared to its premium predecessor, with certain features in the premium version stripped off.

Tesla Switches Strategy in Europe

In recent times, Tesla has relied on releasing more expensive models with more premium features to boost its sales.

The EV maker's launch of the upgraded version of the Model Y SUV earlier in August helped to briefly improve its sales in Europe in September. In China, the EV maker introduced the more premium six-seater Model Y L to Chinese buyers in August amid falling sales in the country. While Tesla's China sales fell in October, they improved last month.

Avoidance and Failure Rate Could Haunt Model 3 Standard

But can relying on a cheaper version of the popular sedan help Tesla win back Europeans? One key obstacle is the high level of Tesla brand avoidance in Europe, especially in Germany and Norway, over CEO Elon Musk's recent political involvement.

Another obstacle is that a newly released report in Germany, which is well-followed in the country and beyond, rated Tesla vehicles as the worst for reliability. The report indicated that while the Model 3 had one of the lowest failure rates of cars mechanically examined for the report, the Model Y's failure rate was much worse. It came in at 17.3%, nearly three times higher than the standard failure rate.

Is Tesla a Buy or Sell?

Wall Street analysts remain cautious of Tesla's shares and still have a Hold consensus rating on the stock. This is based on 13 Buys, 11 Holds, and 10 Sells recommended by 34 analysts over the past three months.

Moreover, at $383.04, the average TSLA price target indicates approximately 16% downside risk.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Alphabet (GOOGL) Enters a New AI Era — What Will It Look Like in 2035?
data.description:
Alphabet (GOOGL) is Google's parent company and one of the biggest names in tech. The company has entered a new phase powered by AI, more cloud use, and steady demand across Search, YouTube, Android, and Workspace. Alphabet is already huge, but the next decade could change how it earns money and how fast it grows.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

In Q3 2025, Alphabet posted more than $100 billion in quarterly revenue for the first time. Sales came in at about $102.3 billion, up 16% from last year. Strong AI-led growth in Google Cloud helped lift results and gave a clearer view of where the company may be heading by 2035.

AI and Cloud Could Lift Alphabet's Next Stage of Growth

Alphabet is moving into a new phase as AI and Cloud take on a bigger role. New AI-related wins, such as the Gemini 3 rollout and reports of Meta (META) exploring Google's TPUs, have strengthened this trend. In Q3 2025, Google Cloud revenue grew 34% year over year to about $15.2 billion, driven by demand for AI training and data tools.

Management said AI is now built into almost every major Google product. This includes Search, YouTube, Chrome, Android, and Workspace. Because these products reach billions of people, Alphabet can roll out AI features at a scale few rivals can match.

If demand for AI stays strong, Cloud could become a bigger part of Alphabet's revenue mix by 2030.

Alphabet May Become More Profitable

Alphabet has focused on efficiency over the past few years. It has trimmed headcount in some areas, reorganized teams, and added more automation. These steps, along with stronger sales, helped lift margins in 2025.

In Q3 2025, Alphabet reported an operating margin of around 30.5%. Excluding a major fine, the margin was close to 33.9%, up from just over 32% a year earlier. Google Cloud margins also improved. Cloud earned around $3.6 billion in operating income in the quarter, with margins in the mid-20% range, up from the high teens in 2024.

If these trends continue, Alphabet could see higher profits over the next few years.

What Alphabet May Look Like in 2035

By 2035, Alphabet will likely look more like an AI-and-cloud company than the ad-heavy business it is today. Google Cloud could take up a much bigger share of revenue as demand for AI training, data processing, and enterprise tools grows. AI will sit at the center of Search, YouTube, Android, and Workspace, helping Alphabet earn more from the same massive user base.

Growth may slow as the company gets bigger, but Alphabet may become steadier, more efficient, and more focused on high-value cloud and AI services.

Is GOOGL Stock a Buy, Hold, or Sell?

Currently, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on Alphabet stock based on 30 Buys and seven Hold recommendations. The average GOOGL stock price target of $317.35 indicates shares are fully priced at current levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Why SoFi Stock Is Trending Lower Today. Is Wall Street Overreacting?
data.description:
SoFi Technologies (SOFI), the digital bank and lending platform, is down again in pre-market trading today after sliding more than 6% in after-hours trade on Thursday. The weakness followed the company's plan to raise $1.5 billion through a new common share sale, a move that caught many traders by surprise.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

SoFi said the offering will bring in cash that will help boost its capital levels and support new growth plans. The underwriter may also get a 30-day option to buy a small extra batch of shares if needed.

The firm has been working to expand its lending and banking services, and the extra funds give it more support as it adds new products. Still, the size and timing of the raise led to a sharp, immediate reaction.

Why Investors Reacted So Strongly

The sell-off was driven by dilution fears. A larger share count reduces the value of current holdings, and that risk tends to show up right away. Fintech stocks have also been facing tight margins and heavy rivalry, so any sign of a new share issue can add pressure.

Even so, some analysts say the slide may be too steep. More capital can help SoFi back new loans, handle swings in funding needs, and keep its growth plan on track.

What Investors Should Look For Next

SoFi has been one of the stronger performers in the market this year. The stock is up 89%, far ahead of the S&P 500's 16.5% rise, helped by firm loan demand and steady growth in fee revenue. This backdrop makes the latest slide stand out.

The next step is to see how SoFi puts the new cash to work. Faster loan growth, steady gains in members, or signs of better profit trends could help ease near-term worry. The company has been adding more tools to its banking and investing app to drive higher user activity.

For now, the stock is under pressure. But if the raise helps support faster growth, the pullback may look more like an overreaction than a warning sign.

Is SOFI Stock a Good Buy Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on SOFI stock based on four Buys, eight Holds, and four Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average SOFI price target of $26.96 per share implies 8.92% downside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: BitMine Pours $130M into Ethereum as Tom Lee Declares $62,000 ETH Price Target
data.description:
Ethereum (ETH-USD) is showing renewed strength, trading above $3,200 after a strategic accumulation move by BitMine Immersion Technologies (BMNR). The company, chaired by renowned analyst Tom Lee, acquired over 41,946 ETH worth $130.78 million at an average price near $3,100 per coin.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Tom Lee believes the crypto market correction that began in October is officially over and that the market has bottomed and is entering a reversal phase. He credits the current recovery to growing institutional interest and real-world adoption, not the traditional four-year Bitcoin halving cycle.

$62,000 ETH Price Target Driven by Institutional Use

Lee remains extremely bullish on Ethereum, emphasizing its growing use by massive financial firms. He specifically named JPMorgan (JPM) and BlackRock (BLK) as institutions building tokenization infrastructure on the network, confirming Ethereum's evolution into the primary settlement layer for global finance.

Lee provided three high-level price targets for ETH based on its historical ratio to Bitcoin:

  • If Ethereum returns to its average historical price ratio to Bitcoin, it could trade around $12,000.
  • A return to the 2021 peak ratio could place ETH near $22,000.
  • Under the most optimistic scenario, where the ETH/BTC ratio reaches 0.25 (and Ethereum becomes a global financial backbone), ETH could reach a staggering $62,000.

$130 Million Bet Confirms Bullish Sentiment

BitMine Immersion Technologies has been strategically increasing its Ethereum holdings during the market recovery. The company's recent acquisition of 41,946 ETH confirms Tom Lee's conviction that the crypto correction has ended. According to a company release, BitMine now holds over $12 billion worth of Ether.

Network metrics are supporting this bullish accumulation. Data from CryptoQuant shows the Taker Buy/Sell Ratio for ETH on Binance has risen to its highest level since August. A breakout of this ratio above 1.0 would officially confirm the end of November's correction and could propel ETH toward the $3,500 and $4,000 levels. Furthermore, the recent Fusaka network upgrade may have contributed to improved sentiment by signaling increased developer activity and institutional confidence.

Key Resistance Levels To Break

To sustain the current upward momentum and bring the higher price targets into play, analysts say Ethereum must clear several key resistance levels. Analyst Ali Martinez stated that the first major targets are $4,800, followed by $6,800, and finally $8,800. These levels must be breached before the extreme target of $62,000 can be realistically considered.

At the time of writing, Ethereum is sitting at $3,131.49.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Rise Ahead of Key Inflation Reading
data.description:
U.S. stock futures moved higher on Friday morning ahead of the release of the Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) price index data, the Federal Reserve's preferred inflation measure, along with other key economic reports. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.32%, 0.19%, and 0.09%, respectively, at 8:18 a.m. EST on December 5.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

During Thursday's regular trading session, the three major indexes closed mixed, as investors digested November's job cuts data, AI-related news, and tariffs. The Dow Jones fell 0.07%, while the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq Composite gained 0.11% and 0.22%, respectively.

Investors await a slew of economic reports today that could shape the Fed's upcoming policy decisions. Focus will be on the delayed September data for consumer spending and incomes, the PCE reading, and the University of Michigan's December consumer sentiment survey.

Turning to trending stocks, Netflix (NFLX) stock was down 2% in pre-market trading on news of its $72 billion acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD). Rubrik (RBRK) stock jumped 18% after the cloud data management company reported market-beating Q3 FY26 results. Shares of Ulta Beauty (ULTA) were up 6%, as the specialty beauty retailer raised its full-year sales guidance.

Meanwhile, SoFi Technologies (SOFI) stock declined more than 7% after the fintech company announced an underwritten public offering of $1.5 billion in shares.

data.title: Musk's X Fined $140M By EU For 'Deceiving Users' With Blue Ticks Breaches
data.description:
The European Commission has fined Elon Musk's social media group X (PC:XAIIQ) €120 million ($140 million) for deceiving users and breaching its Digital Services Act.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Deception, Transparency and Failure

In a ruling today the Commission, after a two year investigation, said X had breached its transparency obligations under the act. The breaches include the deceptive design of its 'blue checkmark', the lack of transparency of its advertising repository, and the failure to provide access to public data for researchers.

The Commission said the blue checkmark deceived users. "On X, anyone can pay to obtain the 'verified' status without the company meaningfully verifying who is behind the account, making it difficult for users to judge the authenticity of accounts and content they engage with.," it said. "This deception exposes users to scams, including impersonation frauds While the DSA does not mandate user verification, it clearly prohibits online platforms from falsely claiming that users have been verified, when no such verification took place."

It added that X incorporates design features and access barriers, such as excessive delays in processing, which undermine the purpose of ad repositories.

Risk of Further Fines

X now has 60 working days to inform the Commission of the specific measures it intends to take to bring to an end the infringement related to the deceptive use of blue checkmarks. It has 90 days for the other two infringements.

Failure to comply, it warned may lead to further fines.

"The DSA restores trust in the online environment. With the DSA's first non-compliance decision, we are holding X responsible for undermining users' rights and evading accountability," said Henna Virkkunen, Executive Vice-President for Tech Sovereignty, Security and Democracy.

The fines broke down into three sections: €45 million for introducing a "verification" blue tick that users could buy, leaving others unable to determine the authenticity of account holders; €35 million for breaches of ad regulations; and €40 million for data access breaches in relation to research.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: Meta Stock Rips Higher as Zuckerberg Slashes Metaverse Budget to Fuel $70 Billion AI Bet Against Apple's Hardware Lead
data.description:
Meta Platforms (META) is effectively admitting defeat in the expensive Metaverse race, instead shifting its entire focus and billions of dollars to lead the next stage of the Artificial Intelligence (AI) trade, which is wearable devices. This radical business transition is being cheered by investors, with the stock climbing over 3% on the news.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

The budget cut proves that CEO Mark Zuckerberg is abandoning his initial vision for virtual worlds, a venture that has resulted in more than $70 billion in operating losses for the Reality Labs division since 2021. The money saved on the Metaverse is now being pumped directly into AI-powered hardware, placing Meta on a collision course with Apple (AAPL).

$70 Billion Failure Fuels AI Glasses Bet

While Meta has struggled to keep pace with rivals like OpenAI and Google in the chatbot race, the company is attempting to leverage its success in hardware where it has a genuine hit with its Ray-Ban branded smart glasses. Analysts view this move as sensible: making AI models is a costly race where a lead is hard to maintain, but success in hardware could prove more durable.

Meta is preparing to slash its metaverse budget by as much as 30% as resources are redirected toward high-momentum projects, particularly AI glasses. Zuckerberg sees these AI-powered glasses as the future "personal superintelligence" that could eventually replace the smartphone.

Apple's Alarm Bells Are Ringing

This shift should sound loud alarm bells at Apple. The iPhone maker has largely stayed out of the AI spending frenzy, which has benefitted its stock, but rivals are aggressively pushing new technology to disrupt the dominance of the smartphone.

Apple is not completely blind to the threat and is planning its own smart glasses. However, its first major product in the space, the Vision Pro (a full virtual-reality headset), looks to have been a disappointment compared to the lightweight glasses Meta is focused on.

Meanwhile, Apple is facing a brain drain, losing top engineers and designers to high-paying competitors, including Jony Ive's work on a mysterious AI device and Meta's recent poaching of former Apple design talent.

investors can compare Meta and Apple's stocks side-by-side based on various financial metrics and analyst ratings on the TipRanks Stocks Comparison Tool. Click on the image below to explore the tool.
data.author: Annika Masrani
